title: Double Piston Extender
description: Moves a block two spaces using two sticky pistons in sequence. The rear piston fires immediately on input; the front piston fires 2 ticks later via a repeater. This ordering pushes the front piston one block forward first, then the rear piston catches up and pushes both the front piston and target another block. Result: the target block moves two spaces total. Reverse by cutting the input signal.

steps.title: Stone base
steps.explanation: Seven stone blocks: four along the main axis (x=0–3, z=0) for the pistons and target block to sit on, and three along the control side (x=−1,0,1 at z=1) for the lever, signal path, and repeater.

steps.title: Rear and front sticky pistons
steps.explanation: Rear sticky piston at (−1,1,0) facing east; front sticky piston at (0,1,0) facing east. Both face the push direction. When extended, the rear pushes the front piston forward; the front then extends further to push the target. Place a yellow wool block at (1,1,0) as the target you want to move.

steps.title: Timed trigger wiring
steps.explanation: Lever at (−1,1,1): directly adjacent to the rear piston so it fires immediately. The lever also feeds a repeater at (0,1,1) facing east with delay=2 — this delayed signal reaches dust at (1,1,1), which powers the front piston two ticks later. Flip the lever: rear fires first, front follows. Flip back: both retract and pull the target home.
